content: The brain's prefrontal-hippocampal interaction during memory retrieval acts as a biological template to resolve working memory capacity bottlenecks in state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents: the prefrontal cortex dynamically gates which hippocampal episodic memories are retrieved to avoid capacity overload during reasoning, which can be translated to a novel gating mechanism for active learning agents. This insight suggests that biologically-inspired adaptive gating of retrieved episodic memories, modeled after prefrontal-hippocampal coordination, will improve the long-horizon reasoning performance of curious active learning agents without linearly increasing computational cost.
